title: Azure Logic Apps - Data Operations Code Samples
applies_to: Logic Apps workflows that transform, shape, filter, or tabulate data in-workflow
Compose, Table (CSV/HTML), Filter array, Join, Parse JSON, and Select actions

Compose.description: Merges multiple values or expressions into a single output token. Useful for building objects or formatted strings from variables before passing them downstream.
key_facts: type is 'Compose'
inputs can be any valid expression, object, or literal — not limited to a single value
Output is referenced as outputs('Compose') in downstream actions

Create_CSV_table.description: Converts a JSON array into a flat CSV string. Each object in the array becomes one row; object keys become column headers.
key_facts: type is 'Table', format is 'CSV'
inputs.from must be an array expression — typically body('ParseJson')?['Results'] for API data
Output is referenced as body('Create_CSV_table') and is a string, not an array

Create_HTML_table.description: Converts a JSON array into an HTML table string. Same shape as CSV table but output is HTML markup.
key_facts: type is 'Table', format is 'HTML'
inputs.from must be an array expression
Output is an HTML string suitable for email bodies (e.g. via Office 365 Outlook connector)

Filter_array.description: Filters an array, keeping only items that match a condition. In code view the action type is 'Query'.
key_facts: type is 'Query' (not 'Filter') in the workflow definition JSON
inputs.from is the source array expression
inputs.where is the condition expression; use item() to reference the current element
Output is referenced as body('Filter_array') and is an array

Join.description: Concatenates all items in an array into a single string, separated by a specified delimiter.
key_facts: type is 'Join'
inputs.from is the source array expression
inputs.joinWith is the delimiter string
Output is a string referenced as body('Join')

Parse_JSON.description: Parses a JSON string or object and generates typed tokens for each property, making fields accessible by name in downstream actions.
key_facts: type is 'ParseJson'
inputs.content is the source — typically body('HTTP_action') or a variable
inputs.schema defines the expected shape; generate from a sample payload using the designer
After parsing, access fields as body('Parse_JSON')?['fieldName'] — use ?['x'] null-safe form
Always parse before accessing nested fields; the raw HTTP body is untyped

Select_array_to_objects.description: Projects each item in an array into a new shape. Example 1: transforms an integer array into an array of objects.
key_facts: type is 'Select'
inputs.from is the source array
inputs.select is an object mapping output field names to expressions; use item() for the current element
Output is always an array with the same item count as the source
body('Select') references the output array

Select_objects_to_strings.description: Projects each item in a JSON object array into a string (scalar) rather than another object. Requires text mode in the designer or code view — the select value is a plain expression string, not an object.
key_facts: When select is a string expression (not an object), the output is an array of strings
Use @{item()?['FieldName']} as the select value to extract a single field from each object
This mode is not available in the designer map view — switch to text mode (T icon) or use code view
The from expression typically references an OData-style value array: @body('action')?['value']

implementation_notes: Filter array uses type 'Query' in JSON code view, not 'Filter'. Do not confuse the designer label with the JSON type.
Parse JSON must precede any action that accesses typed fields from an API response. Raw HTTP body references are strings, not objects.
Use the null-safe ?['field'] accessor form for all body() and variables() expressions where the field might be absent — especially for API error response fields.
The Table action (CSV or HTML) takes an array as input and produces a flat string. It cannot handle nested objects well — flatten with Select first if the source array contains nested objects.
Select with a string select value (for array-to-string-array projection) is only editable in code view or text mode. The visual designer map interface only supports object-to-object projection.
Compose is useful for pre-building a payload object before an HTTP call or SharePoint write, avoiding repeated variable references in the target action inputs.
Join is the correct action for turning an array into a delimited string (e.g., comma-separated IDs). Do not use Compose or a ForEach for this.
InitializeVariable actions are only valid in the workflow definition's actions block — they cannot be placed inside a Foreach or Until scope in Consumption workflows.
common_failures: Using type 'Filter' instead of 'Query' for Filter array actions — causes validation error.
Referencing body() fields without a preceding Parse JSON — the expression returns null or errors at runtime.
Passing a nested object array directly to Create CSV table without flattening — produces '[object Object]' in CSV cells.
Using the object select form when trying to project to a string array — results in an array of single-key objects instead.
Calling outputs('Compose') when the correct reference for downstream HTTP or SharePoint input is simply @outputs('Compose') — note it is outputs() not body() for Compose.
